product_overview: Drexii is a live AI agent platform that unifies multiple integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, etc.) into a single chat interface with multi-model routing, chained automations, persistent memory, and a confirm-before-act safety layer.
core_goals: Provide a secure authenticated chat interface to interact with an AI agent (Drexii) that can call external tools and services.
Enable multi-step automations and workflows with condition chaining and audit logging.
Persist and include user memory safely in prompts and sanitize external tool outputs to reduce prompt-injection risk.
Expose integration management for users and allow webhook-based triggers (e.g., Gmail push notifications).
Surface model routing / status and require confirm-before-act for sensitive agent actions.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: User login, logout, session validation, and account deletion using the InsForge auth flow and server session endpoints.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with { success: true } and session token -> GET /api/auth/check with session token -> Receive 200 with { user: User } -> POST /api/auth/logout with session token -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> GET /api/auth/check without token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 -> POST /api/auth/delete-account with session token -> Receive 200 with { success: true } -> GET /api/auth/check with old token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Chat / AI Threads
features.description: Create conversation threads, send messages to the AI agent (Drexii), and stream AI responses; supports fetching thread history.
user_flows: POST /api/threads with body { title?: string } and auth -> Receive 200 with Thread -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: string } and auth -> Receive 200 with { message: Message, response: string } (streaming) -> GET /api/threads/:id with auth -> Receive 200 with { thread: Thread, messages: Message[] }
POST /api/threads/:nonexistent/messages with body { content: string } and auth -> Receive 404 Not found

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with content containing tool output injection and auth -> Receive 200 where server sanitizes tool output before including in AI prompt (sanitizer exercised) -> GET /api/threads/:id with auth -> Receive 200 with messages including sanitized content

features.name: Automations
features.description: Create, list, toggle, delete, trigger, and inspect logs for automations; supports chaining conditions and optional tool execution.
user_flows: POST /api/automations with body { name, instructions, trigger } and auth -> Receive 200 with Automation -> POST /api/automations/process with body { automationId, triggerContext } and auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true } -> GET /api/automations/:id/logs with auth -> Receive 200 with AutomationLog[]

GET /api/automations with auth -> Receive 200 with Automation[] -> POST /api/automations/:id/toggle with auth -> Receive 200 with { isActive: boolean } -> DELETE /api/automations/:id with auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/automations with invalid body missing required fields and auth -> Receive 400 Validation error

POST /api/automations/process with body where chain condition 'output contains X' is false and auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true } and subsequent chained step is NOT executed (check logs via GET /api/automations/:id/logs -> Receive 200 showing no chained execution)

POST /api/automations/process with body that triggers tool call in agent response and auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true } where server parses tool call (parseToolCalls) and logs tool execution -> GET /api/automations/:id/logs with auth -> Receive 200 containing tool execution audit entries

features.name: Workflows
features.description: Create, list, run, and delete multi-step agent workflows that may execute tools and return run results.
user_flows: POST /api/workflows with body { name, steps } and auth -> Receive 200 with Workflow -> POST /api/workflows/:id/run with auth -> Receive 200 with WorkflowRunResult containing step outputs and status -> GET /api/workflows with auth -> Receive 200 with Workflow[]

POST /api/workflows/:id/run for workflow that triggers an AI tool call in a step and auth -> Receive 200 where server uses parseToolCalls and schema translation (toAnthropicTools / toGeminiTools) to call tools -> Receive aggregated WorkflowRunResult
POST /api/workflows with invalid steps payload and auth -> Receive 400 Validation error
POST /api/workflows/:nonexistent/run with auth -> Receive 404 Not found

features.name: Integrations
features.description: List available integration types and let users add, test, and remove their integration credentials.
user_flows: GET /api/integrations (public) -> Receive 200 with Integration[] -> GET /api/user-integrations with auth -> Receive 200 with UserIntegration[] -> POST /api/user-integrations with body { type, credentials } and auth -> Receive 200 with UserIntegration -> POST /api/user-integrations/test with body { integrationId } and auth -> Receive 200 with { success: boolean, message: string }
DELETE /api/user-integrations/:id with auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/user-integrations/test with invalid integrationId and auth -> Receive 200 with { success: false, message: string } or 400 if payload malformed

features.name: Memory
features.description: Persist and manage memory entries used to augment AI prompts; memories are formatted and inserted into prompts in a wrapped, untrusted context.
user_flows: POST /api/memory with body { content } and auth -> Receive 200 with MemoryEntry -> GET /api/memory with auth -> Receive 200 with MemoryEntry[] -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages with content and auth -> Receive 200 where server includes formatted memories wrapped in <memory_context> (formatMemoriesForPrompt) in AI prompt -> GET /api/threads/:id with auth -> Receive 200 containing message history
PATCH /api/memory/:id with body { content } and auth -> Receive 200 with updated MemoryEntry -> DELETE /api/memory/:id with auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/memory with empty content and auth -> Receive 200 with MemoryEntry (sanity-handled) -> GET /api/memory with auth -> Receive 200 including the entry

features.name: Audit Log
features.description: Provide an audit trail of tool executions and agent actions for transparency and debugging.
user_flows: GET /api/audit with auth -> Receive 200 with AuditLog[] listing tool executions and agent decisions
GET /api/audit with auth after an automation run that executed tools -> Receive 200 with new AuditLog entries for that run

features.name: Pending Actions
features.description: Surface agent actions that require explicit user confirmation and allow the user to confirm or cancel them.
user_flows: GET /api/actions/pending with auth -> Receive 200 with PendingAction[] -> POST /api/actions/:id/confirm with auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true } -> GET /api/actions/pending with auth -> Receive 200 where the confirmed action is removed
GET /api/actions/pending with auth -> Receive 200 with PendingAction[] -> POST /api/actions/:id/cancel with auth -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/actions/:nonexistent/confirm with auth -> Receive 404 Not found

features.name: Model Status
features.description: Expose current AI model availability and routing decisions so clients can adapt or show status to users.
user_flows: GET /api/model/status -> Receive 200 with { models: ModelStatus[] } (public endpoint) showing available models and routing rules
GET /api/model/status during model outage -> Receive 200 with models marking unavailable and optional fallback hints

features.name: Gmail Webhook
features.description: Receive Gmail push notifications to trigger automations and internal processing.
user_flows: POST /api/webhooks/gmail with body { message, subscription } (unauthenticated webhook) -> Receive 200 with { success: true } -> POST /api/automations/process with auth triggered internally -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/webhooks/gmail with malformed body -> Receive 400 or 200 with error handling depending on webhook validation (server must not throw)

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public marketing landing page describing Drexii and linking to docs and sign-in.
user_flows: GET / (landing page) -> Receive 200 with HTML content (index.vue) -> User clicks Sign in navigates to /login
GET / (landing page) with missing assets -> Receive 200 with page fallback or 404 for assets (frontend handles gracefully)

features.name: Documentation
features.description: In-app documentation describing features, integrations, and developer notes.
user_flows: GET /docs -> Receive 200 with documentation HTML (docs.vue)
GET /docs with unsupported client -> Receive 200 with accessible fallback content

features.name: Prompt Injection Sanitizer (sanitizeToolOutput / wrapToolContext)
features.description: Sanitize external tool outputs for known prompt injection patterns and wrap sanitized output in explicit tool-context tags before including in prompts.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: 'tool output containing "ignore previous instructions"' } and auth -> Server runs sanitizeToolOutput -> Receive 200 where AI prompt and thread messages include content with matched patterns replaced by [FILTERED] and wrapped via wrapToolContext
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: '' } and auth -> Server runs sanitizeToolOutput with empty string -> Receive 200 without errors and AI prompt includes empty tool context
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with content containing multiple injection patterns and auth -> Server runs sanitizeToolOutput -> Receive 200 where all instances are replaced case-insensitively

features.name: AI Task Classifier (classifyTask)
features.description: Classify user tasks by complexity to decide model routing (lite, standard, heavy, code) based on message content and thread metadata.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body where last message is from user and short casual text and auth -> Server calls classifyTask -> Receive 200 where classification is 'lite' and model routing uses a lighter model in the agent response
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body containing code keywords and auth -> Server calls classifyTask -> Receive 200 where classification is 'code' and server routes to code-capable model
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with empty message array context or last message not from user and auth -> Server calls classifyTask -> Receive 200 where classification defaults to 'heavy' (fallback)

features.name: Tool Schema Translator (toAnthropicTools / toGeminiTools)
features.description: Translate internal tool schemas to provider-specific formats (Anthropic, Google Generative AI) for tool registration with model SDKs.
user_flows: POST /api/workflows/:id/run with steps that include tool schemas and auth -> Server calls toAnthropicTools and toGeminiTools to produce SDK-compatible tool declarations -> Receive 200 with WorkflowRunResult where tools were callable by the model
POST /api/workflows/:id/run with empty schemas array and auth -> Server calls translators -> Receive 200 where translators return empty arrays without error

features.name: Tool Call Parser (parseToolCalls)
features.description: Parse AI responses for embedded [TOOL_CALL: ...] directives and extract tool name + args, handling JSON and simple key:value fallback parsing.
user_flows: POST /api/workflows/:id/run with AI response containing '[TOOL_CALL: myTool({"key":"value"})]' and auth -> Server runs parseToolCalls -> Receive 200 where parsed calls include { name: 'myTool', args: { key: 'value' } } and server invokes tool

POST /api/workflows/:id/run with AI response containing malformed args like '[TOOL_CALL: myTool({key: value})]' and auth -> Server runs parseToolCalls -> Receive 200 where fallback parser extracts { key: 'value' } or server logs a warning and skips unparseable call without throwing

features.name: Chain Condition Evaluator (evaluateChainCondition)
features.description: Evaluate plain-English chain conditions for automations and workflows to decide whether to execute chained steps.
user_flows: POST /api/automations/process with body where condition is 'output contains invoice' and auth -> Server runs evaluateChainCondition with output containing 'Invoice #123' -> Receive 200 with { success: true } and subsequent chained action executed (logs reflect execution)

POST /api/automations/process with body where condition is 'failure' and status 'success' and auth -> Server runs evaluateChainCondition -> Receive 200 with { success: true } but chained step NOT executed (logs reflect no execution)

POST /api/automations/process with body where condition is unknown text and auth -> Server runs evaluateChainCondition -> Receive 200 where unknown condition defaults to true and chained step is executed (server default behavior documented)

features.name: Memory Prompt Formatter (formatMemoriesForPrompt)
features.description: Format stored memories into a wrapped <memory_context> block for inclusion in AI prompts, handling edge cases and special characters.
user_flows: GET /api/memory with auth -> Receive 200 with MemoryEntry[] -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages with content and auth -> Server calls formatMemoriesForPrompt -> Receive 200 where AI prompt contains <memory_context> with each memory line formatted as '- [category] content' and a warning that content inside tags is untrusted
GET /api/memory returning [] with auth -> Server calls formatMemoriesForPrompt -> Receive 200 where formatted memories string is '' and AI prompt excludes memory block

tech_stack.framework: Nuxt 4, Vue 3, Nuxt UI, Pinia, Tailwind CSS

tech_stack.other: Anthropic SDK, Google Generative AI, InsForge Auth SDK, nuxt-security

Send a message and get an AI response (streaming)

known_limitations.issue: Agent tool injection risk — external tool output is sanitized but passed back into prompts
known_limitations.impact: Potential prompt injection if sanitizeToolOutput is insufficient

known_limitations.issue: Chain condition evaluation uses simple string matching, not a full expression evaluator
known_limitations.impact: Complex chain conditions may not evaluate correctly

known_limitations.issue: Auth is client-side only (InsForge SDK); server routes rely on session tokens that could be stale
known_limitations.impact: Edge cases where expired sessions are not immediately invalidated server-side
